2026.Name: Food & Ideas Festival
2026.About: The second annual festival expanded to include a student start-up food market, a school outreach programme, and a live cook-off between faculty heads. Ticket pre-sales exceeded last year's total attendance within 48 hours of opening.

2026.Name: Love & Dough: Valentine's Doughnut Design-Off
2026.About: Students competed to create the most creative glazed doughnut in honour of Valentine's Day. Entries ranged from anatomically accurate heart-shaped glazes to a surprisingly moving abstract design titled 'Long Distance.' 900 doughnuts were made and sold.

2026.Name: Olive Oil Masterclass
2026.About: A sold-out tasting and cooking workshop led by a visiting producer from Crete. Participants sampled 12 single-origin oils and learned to cook three dishes pairing different varieties. Waiting list of 180 for the next session.

2025.Name: Bearcat Dining
2025.About: The National University Catering Association awarded Lander University its highest honour, citing food sustainability initiatives, cultural diversity of menus, and student co-creation programmes. Head of Dining Services, Lander University, accepted the award at the ceremony.

2025.Name: Bearcat Farmers' Market
2025.About: The monthly market celebrated its third year and announced expansion to a weekly format, with 6 new permanent student vendor licences created to support student food entrepreneurs. Total revenue passed $120,000 since launch.

2025.Name: Snap & Savour: Student Food Photography Contest
2025.About: Students submitted food photos taken on campus — dishes cooked at home, café finds, market hauls. Over 600 entries were received. The winning series, 'Breakfast at 6am,' depicted revision-week meals with striking honesty.

2024.Name: Frozen Dessert Design Showcase
2024.About: The Art & Design faculty collaborated with the Culinary Society to create edible sculptural ice cream — flavours included Earl Grey & violet, brown butter miso, and black sesame tahini. A public vote selected the summer menu for the campus café.

2024.Name: Big Soup Sunday
2024.About: A charity soup-a-thon running from 10am to 10pm. Volunteers made and served 48 different soups continuously, raising $8,500 for student hardship funds. The event sold out of bread rolls by noon.

2023.Name: Student Hot Sauce Competition
2023.About: Students submitted homemade hot sauces judged on heat, flavour complexity, and label design. 'Ghost of Deadline Past' by a 2nd-year Chemistry student won all three categories and is now stocked in the campus shop.

2023.Name: The Lander Community Cookbook, Vol. 1
2023.About: Three years in the making: a 120-recipe cookbook compiled entirely from student, staff and alumni submissions. Recipes span 47 countries of origin. All proceeds funded scholarships for food science students from underrepresented backgrounds.

2023.Name: Noodle Competition
2023.About: Seven universities sent student teams to compete in a noodle-making relay race combining speed, technique, and flavour. Lander University's own 'Ramen Raiders' placed second.

2022.Name: Lander University Night Market Launch
2022.About: A new monthly street food market opened on campus, run jointly with Elmwood Borough Council and local food entrepreneurs. Student food business pitches were featured alongside established vendors.

2022.Name: Inter-Faculty Bake-Off Championships
2022.About: Twelve faculties, twelve cakes. Engineering's structurally ambitious seven-tier gravity cake collapsed spectacularly during judging but won the crowd's heart. Law School took the trophy with a near-perfect pistachio entremet.

2022.Name: Plant Week: A Whole Week of Vegan Dining
2022.About: All campus cafeterias went 100% plant-based for seven days as part of the university's sustainability pledge. Daily themed menus ('Mediterranean Monday', 'Street Food Saturday') drew record footfall to the dining halls

2021.Name: Taco Fiesta & Fusion Fair
2021.About: Lander students constructed a continuous baguette sandwich measuring 47.3 metres — filling: smoked salmon, crème fraîche, and capers. Verified by two independent stewards. The sandwich was donated to a local shelter.

2021.Name: Welcome Back
2021.About: A two-week intensive run by the Food Science department covering South Carolina. Limited to 40 participants, it sold out in under 6 minutes. A waitlist of 300+ prompted a repeat in 2022.

2021.Name: World Record Attempt: Longest Student-Made Sandwich
2021.About: World Record Attempt: Longest Student-Made Sandwich Lander students constructed a continuous baguette sandwich measuring 47.3 metres — filling: smoked salmon, crème fraîche, and capers. Verified by two independent stewards. The sandwich was donated to a local shelter.
